title: Brad Pitt’s ‘F1’ Trailer Hype, Mia Goth’s ‘MaXXXine’ Madness, and ‘Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F’
format: Sean and Amanda open on the F1 trailer and the Skydance-Paramount deal, then argue over MaXXXine and the whole Ty West trilogy. Van Lathan joins for Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F, Sean reports back on Despicable Me 4, and Ty West closes it out in conversation.

films.title: F1
films.note: The Apple/Brad Pitt racing movie, originally titled Apex. The trailer used Queen's 'We Will Rock You'; Amanda pledged to learn nothing about the sport before release.

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: Amanda's read on the F1 trailer: it opens on a Brad Pitt/Kerry Condon exchange that plays like a Billy Beane scene with the bite taken out for a Top Gun crowd.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Sean's benchmark for how much he wants to know about F1 going in: none. 'Tom Cruise must be really pissed.'

films.title: The Beekeeper
films.note: First up on Amanda's husband's flight, before he cut to Moneyball with an hour left.

films.title: Casablanca
films.note: Hugh Grant marked the French election by posting the film's 'La Marseillaise' scene.

films.title: MaXXXine
films.note: Third in the trilogy, set amid the Night Stalker summer of 1985 L.A. Sean's least favorite of the three but a success on its own terms; a dense stew of references that Amanda found harder to enjoy without placing every one. Both felt the third act loses steam and mutes Mia Goth.

films.title: X
films.note: The 1970s Texas original about strippers and filmmakers shooting a porno at a cabin. Sean: Pearl is part of why X grew into a cult classic.

films.title: Pearl
films.note: The Technicolor prequel about X's killer as a young woman in 1918. Amanda's favorite of the three, for the Wizard of Oz nods; Sean calls the held final smile a big, weird stroke.

films.title: A Cure for Wellness
films.note: Sean's example of Mia Goth going bonkers, a Gore Verbinski movie.

films.title: Body Double
films.note: The De Palma touchstone Amanda has seen, so the direct references landed for her.

films.title: Psycho
films.note: An overt part of the stew; Amanda's breaking point was the golf-cart 'this is where Psycho was filmed' beat with the Bates Motel sign filling the screen.

films.title: Hardcore
films.note: A major reference point once Maxine sets out to remake hardcore in 1980s L.A.

films.title: Pretty Woman
films.note: Amanda's comfort-zone comparison for the L.A.-striver material.

films.title: Sunset Boulevard
films.note: The Hollywood-climber lineage Amanda is drawn to over the video-store slashers.

films.title: All About Eve
films.note: Sean's frame for the Debicki/Goth pairing that never quite arrives, two generations of women.

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: Part of the 'people trying to climb' canon Sean says the film replicates well.

films.title: The Player
films.note: Named alongside All About Eve and Sunset Boulevard as movies-about-Hollywood the film draws on.

films.title: Vice Squad
films.note: One of the sleazy 1980s L.A. B-movies Sean says you have to watch under the right circumstances.

films.title: Angel
films.note: Sean's litmus test: fire up Angel cold and 'this kind of sucks' unless you're along for the ride. Amanda won't be firing up Tubi for it.

films.title: Fade to Black
films.note: Named with Hardcore in Sean's list of the sub-genre a viewer needs to like for MaXXXine to fully work.

films.title: Tenebre
films.note: Cited with Hardcore and Fade to Black as the kind of movie you need affection for going in.

films.title: Death Proof
films.note: Sean's Tarantino example of the effortless steal, versus West pointing a flashing red sign at his references. He asked Tarantino about a lifted line; 'I didn't borrow it, I stole it.'

films.title: Telefon
films.note: The Charles Bronson / Don Siegel movie Tarantino stole a Death Proof line from, which Sean never would have caught.

films.title: Two Weeks Notice
films.note: Sean's needle at Amanda over B-movies with A ideas; she still hasn't seen it.

films.title: A Family Affair
films.note: Amanda's counter for a B-rom-com she'll defend, also a movie about making movies.

films.title: Longlegs
films.note: Out the following week from Oz Perkins, Anthony Perkins's son, arriving right after a movie so overtly about his father's legacy.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: Among the old Technicolor musicals Pearl pastiches.

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F
films.note: The Netflix legacy sequel, Eddie Murphy's first in 30 years, directed by Mark Molloy. Van compares it to Big3 basketball: enjoyable if you let it be. Everyone returns; the daughter (Taylour Paige) exists to get Axel back to Beverly Hills. Muted, but a good time; Murphy never fully lets himself out of the cage.

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop
films.note: The Simpson/Bruckheimer template, high stakes that are actually low, Van's 'Joe Johnson on the Nets' original.

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop II
films.note: Van's a Beverly Hills Cop II guy; the second film everyone still counts.

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop III
films.note: The PG-13 one without the Axel F score that the house doesn't acknowledge.

films.title: Coming to America
films.note: The Murphy role Van and Sean discussed on an earlier show.

films.title: Coming 2 America
films.note: Van: just not good, smaller and less important; Axel F at least still feels like a Beverly Hills Cop movie.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
films.note: The Shia LaBeouf hand-off that didn't take; audiences wanted Harrison Ford.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: The Phoebe Waller-Bridge attempt to pass the torch, same result.

films.title: Dirty Harry
films.note: Sean's counterexample: Eastwood never came back 40 years later to play the hits.

films.title: The Terminator
films.note: Part of Van's ongoing full-series rewatch and the T-800-returns-no-matter-what pattern.

films.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
films.note: The scary T-1000, Robert Patrick.

films.title: Terminator 3: Rise of the Machines
films.note: Kristanna Loken's T-X, the one Van blanks on the number for.

films.title: Terminator Salvation
films.note: The Christian Bale entry, the only one without Arnold, though the T-800 still shows up.

films.title: Terminator: Dark Fate
films.note: Van's favorite of the recent ones, Mackenzie Davis and a returning Sarah Connor; he'd watch another tomorrow.

films.title: Conan the Barbarian
films.note: Van floats a possible new Arnold Conan; not sure it's real.

films.title: The Virgin Suicides
films.note: Amanda's dream legacy sequel per Van, 'what happened the next day.'

films.title: A Few Good Men
films.note: Van's pick, though Sean's wary of Aaron Sorkin writing Caffee running for governor of Michigan right now.

films.title: Molly's Game
films.note: The Sorkin movie Amanda kept out of the Kevin Costner Hall of Fame.

films.title: WarGames
films.note: Van's real legacy-sequel pitch: bring back Matthew Broderick, a young hacker sent to pull him out of the government. One problem, they just lost Dabney Coleman.

films.title: Harlem Nights
films.note: Sean's alternate ask: have Eddie come back to it.

films.title: Cloak & Dagger
films.note: Van's favorite Dabney Coleman, seen for the first time recently.

films.title: Nine to Five
films.note: Floated as the maybe-title while Sean tried to place a Dabney Coleman comedy.

films.title: Short Time
films.note: The Dabney Coleman movie Sean was actually reaching for, where he thinks he has 24 hours to live.

films.title: Despicable Me 4
films.note: Sean saw it alone at the Americana at 10 p.m. and walked out feeling life is meaningless. Not for three-year-olds; he'll recap the plot at the live show. $230M worldwide over the weekend.

films.title: Despicable Me
films.note: The one Amanda has actually seen; she argues the Despicable Me films, unlike Minions, don't work silent.

films.title: Minions
films.note: Amanda has seen it, but without sound, and still got the comedy, which is the point of the franchise.

films.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
films.note: The other Minions entry Amanda has caught.

films.title: Inside Out 2
films.note: $1.3B and counting; Sean's evidence the box office is basically fine, down just 17% on the year.

films.title: MaXXXine
films.note: West on scaling up for 1980s L.A. after two farm-set movies, keeping the ending secret, and packing in dense subtle Easter eggs (the cocaine in a goose, the nurse outfit) more than the obvious Body Double nods. He set it in the Night Stalker/PMRC summer of 1985 for the maximum real-and-narrative threat, and left the door open to more.

films.title: X
films.note: West wanted Maxine to not repeat X or Pearl; early drafts leaned on callbacks and fell flat fast.

films.title: Pearl
films.note: Born of pandemic circumstance in New Zealand; its big committed visual swing (paint it that red) freed West up going forward. He pitched A24 all three at once, half-joking about X / XX / XXX.

films.title: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre
films.note: The comparison West couldn't avoid on X, people in a van at a farm, even though the movies share little story.

films.title: Body Double
films.note: The kind of overt reference people fixate on; West says far more effort went into the subtle stuff.

films.title: Vice Squad
films.note: A movie West would point an actor to for the feel of 1980s Hollywood; he'd love it to be a gateway film.

films.title: Angel
films.note: West's example of a forgotten, hard-to-find movie that shouldn't just vanish, '500 doesn't get you an El Dorado.'

films.title: The Player
films.note: The kind of movie-business commentary West wanted to thread with 80s action.

films.title: Bram Stoker's Dracula
films.note: One of the vampire movies West lists when riffing on how a sub-genre's watchable titles thin out fast, the seed of a maybe-someday idea.

films.title: The Lost Boys
films.note: Another on the short list of great vampire movies before the well runs dry.

films.title: Interview with the Vampire
films.note: Rounds out West's vampire example; Sean teases him into 'announcing' a vampire movie he insists he doesn't have.

films.title: The Bikeriders
films.note: West hasn't seen it yet but likes Jeff Nichols's work.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: West's confession, still hasn't seen it: 'I'm part of the problem.'

films: A Good Day to Die Hard

Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Cry.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull

Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny

A Good Day to Die Hard.title: A Good Day to Die Hard
